explicit_text: I enlisted in the United States Navy along with two friends because we wanted to ride instead of walk. We went to Dallas for our examination and then to San Diego for boot camp. The Navy was different from other branches; for example, we had to buy our own clothes and initially earned less pay than later. I served aboard several battleships and was part of the crew during critical moments like the attack on Pearl Harbor and later in the Pacific theater during World War II.

explicit_text: Dallas was where my group and I went to take our Navy examinations before boot camp. It was the first step in our journey into the service. I remember being a bit excited and nervous, but we were accepted and sent on to San Diego. That was the start of my Navy career during World War II.
implicit_text: That city in Texas was where my group and I went to take our naval examinations before boot camp. It was the first step in our journey into the service. I remember being a bit excited and nervous, but we were accepted and sent on to the training center in California. That was the start of my naval career during the global conflict.

explicit_text: San Diego was where I went for boot camp after passing the examination in Dallas. We were told not to bring any clothes because the Navy would issue us uniforms and our old clothes would be given to the Salvation Army. Boot camp lasted eight weeks, and I was lucky not to have to do kitchen patrol duties. After training, I was sent to Long Beach to join the USS California.

explicit_text: I served in the United States Navy during World War II, starting before the attack on Pearl Harbor and continuing through the Pacific campaigns. My brothers also served during the war. I was aboard several battleships, including the USS California and later the USS West Virginia, and participated in significant battles such as the Philippines landings and defending against kamikaze attacks.

explicit_text: After completing boot camp in San Diego, I had to go up to Long Beach, California, to join the USS California. It was there that I received my shots and uniforms before boarding the ship. Long Beach was an important transition point from training to active duty aboard a battleship.
implicit_text: After completing boot camp in California, I had to go up to a port city in Southern California to join a battleship named after a state. explicit_text: I was assigned to the USS California after boot camp, and we left for Hawaii on October 1st. On December 7th, 1941, during the attack on Pearl Harbor, I was on the California and had the 4-8 afternoon watch. The ship was hit by torpedoes and bombs, but we managed to keep it from rolling over by flooding the opposite side. We evacuated and returned multiple times during the attack, and the Nevada helped protect us by running aground to block the channel.

explicit_text: I was stationed at Pearl Harbor aboard the USS California in the months leading up to the attack on December 7th, 1941. During the attack, I was on watch and witnessed the chaos as our ship was hit by torpedoes and bombs. We evacuated to the nearby island and then returned to fight fires and man anti-aircraft guns. The attack was devastating, with many ships damaged or sunk, including the Arizona and Oklahoma.

explicit_text: On December 7th, 1941, I was on the USS California during the Attack on Pearl Harbor. I was on the 4-8 watch when the Japanese planes struck, and our ship took multiple torpedo and bomb hits. We evacuated to the island nearby and then returned to fight fires and defend the ship with anti-aircraft guns. The attack was sudden and terrifying, and I remember the chaos and the bravery of the crew as we tried to save the ship and ourselves.

explicit_text: During the attack on Pearl Harbor, the USS Oklahoma was hit and rolled over after being struck by torpedoes. I remember that the California flooded the opposite side to keep from rolling over like the Oklahoma did. The Oklahoma's loss was a stark reminder of how dangerous the attack was and how close we were to similar fate.

explicit_text: The USS Nevada played a crucial role during the Pearl Harbor attack by running aground a few hundred yards from us to keep from blocking the channel. This action helped protect the USS California and others. The Nevada was the only battleship to get underway during the attack, and its sacrifice saved lives and kept the harbor from being completely blocked.

explicit_text: After the attack on Pearl Harbor, I was transferred to the USS West Virginia in 1943 while it was being repaired and refitted. I stayed on the West Virginia until the end of the war. The ship was heavily damaged during the attack but was rebuilt and returned to service. I have a picture of the West Virginia after it was redone, showing the upgrades like the quad 40-millimeter guns.

explicit_text: During the Pearl Harbor attack, the USS Maryland was in port and took a bomb hit but was not vulnerable to torpedoes. The Maryland was part of the battleship group that suffered damage but was quickly repaired to return to action.

explicit_text: The USS Tennessee was also hit during the Pearl Harbor attack; a bomb struck the middle gun turret. The shrapnel from that hit killed the captain of the USS West Virginia. The Tennessee was pulled out after the attack as part of the effort to clear the harbor and repair the fleet.

explicit_text: I heard the USS Arizona blew up during the attack on Pearl Harbor, though I didn't realize what had happened at first. The explosion was massive and devastating, and it was one of the greatest losses that day. The Arizona's destruction was a symbol of the attack's severity and the lives lost—around 2,600 people were killed that day overall.

explicit_text: The Imperial Japanese Navy launched the surprise attack on Pearl Harbor on December 7th, 1941. They used aircraft carriers and two-man submarines, some of which ran aground due to poor navigation. Their attack caused massive damage to our battleships and naval facilities, marking the United States' entry into World War II.
implicit_text: The naval forces of the empire in the Pacific launched the surprise attack on the naval base in Hawaii on December 7th, 1941. They used aircraft carriers and two-man submarines, some of which ran aground due to poor navigation. Their attack caused massive damage to our battleships and naval facilities, marking the United States' entry into the global conflict.

explicit_text: Later in the war, we went to the Philippines where the Navy was heavily involved in supporting the landings. We could see ships everywhere, and we used our 16-inch guns to support the army by taking out targets they called for. General Douglas MacArthur was brought ashore there with reporters to document the event, which was a significant moment in the Pacific campaign.

explicit_text: During the Philippines campaign, General Douglas MacArthur was brought ashore from our fleet along with newsreel reporters. They had to redo the filming because the first attempt wasn't good enough. MacArthur's arrival was a big morale boost for us and symbolized the turning point in the Pacific war.

explicit_text: After some of the battles, we moved out into the China Sea during a storm. We had a couple of destroyers with us, but they ran out of fuel and we couldn't rescue them. The waves were huge, sometimes reaching 40 to 50 feet, and it made me glad I wasn't on one of the smaller destroyers but rather on a battleship like the West Virginia.

explicit_text: The USS Pennsylvania was in dry dock during the Pearl Harbor attack and was spared the worst damage. However, two smaller ships in dry dock in front of her were destroyed, and the carriers tied up nearby were sunk. The Pennsylvania was lucky to avoid major damage, which helped preserve some of our battleship strength after the attack.
